Une aptitude significative à la communication et au travail d'équipe, tout en faisant preuve d'un fort sens du service à la clientèle.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
L'audit digital consiste à analyser et à optimiser les processus commerciaux et informatiques de nos client·e·s, afin de les aider à créer une valeur durable pour un avenir digital. Notre travail se résume à instaurer la confiance : aider les client·e·s à comprendre les avantages – et pas seulement les risques – qu'offrent les nouvelles technologies et leur donner les connaissances et la confiance nécessaires pour confier à des esprits brillants le soin de mener à bien leur transformation technologique.

Évaluation des contrôles internes des processus métier et informatique lors de l'audit des comptes annuels de clients nationaux et internationaux, dans le cadre de l'approche " Audit 4.0 ".
Analyse des transactions, des données de base et des comptes à l'aide d'outils pour détecter les irrégularités, contribution aux projets de " process mining ".
Participation active aux activités de conseil visant l'optimisation des systèmes de contrôle interne et la gestion des risques.
Collaboration à des projets spéciaux liés à l'informatique et aux processus pour nos clients.
Évolution au sein d'un environnement de travail dynamique, favorisant l'esprit d'équipe, la confiance et le soutien mutuel.
Réception régulière de retours constructifs de la part des collègues et des supérieurs.
Potentielles opportunités d'embauche en tant qu'Associate par la suite.

Titulaire, ou en voie de l'être, d'un Bachelor HES (informatique de gestion) ou d'un Master (systèmes d'information)
De langue maternelle française avec d'excellentes compétences en anglais.
Excellentes capacités de synthèse et d'analyse.
Doté(e) d'une grande volonté d'apprendre, avec comme objectif de poursuivre la formation en vue d'obtenir le diplôme d'auditeur CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor).
Une aptitude significative à la communication et au travail d'équipe, tout en faisant preuve d'un fort sens du service à la clientèle.

jobs.title: Manager / Senior Manager Transaction Services
jobs.description:
In Transaction Services you will be at the forefront of our client's M&A activity and lead projects and multidisciplinary, first in class teams.
We are looking for a motivated team player who can help us deliver outstanding client projects and advance our team. A career within the Deals Transaction Services team will provide you with the opportunity to help clients achieve maximum value with their M&A activities. We focus on evaluating M&A projects in due diligence, pre and post completion. We conduct pre deal and confirmatory due diligence, execute large-scale enterprise wide integrations, and support complex divestitures and separations on a wide range of areas.

Help our clients with dedicated support for transactions in a wide variety of industries and circumstances (acquisitions, divestitures, joint ventures & structured investments).
Work across the areas of buy-side due diligence, sell-side assistance and vendor due diligence, deal and negotiation support for both corporate and private equity clients.
Collaborate with other experts to develop a rapid understanding of the client's business and issues, and articulate your findings appropriately in order to deliver high quality reports.
Work in a diversified, international environment covering multiple areas and industries.
Team up with a network of highly talented and knowledgeable experts.

Bachelor's or master's degree and have a background in finance (CPA or CFA).
Thorough understanding of financial accounting and reporting requirements in accordance with internationally recognized reporting standards (e.g. IFRS, US GAAP).
At least 5 years of experience in providing financial due diligence and other transaction-related services to large companies and private equity fund clients.
Fluent in English, any other language is a plus.
You are energetic, proactive, able to empathise with clients and staff, and are enthusiastic about delivering value to clients.
Flexible and demonstrate willingness to conduct business travel for client assignments or business-related matters.
Excellent analytical skills and are experienced in using Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and additional analytical software tools (e.g. PowerBI, Alteryx) to analyse and present data in a meaningful and efficient way.

jobs.title: Senior Associate / Manager Financial Services Consulting - Liquidity & Funding
jobs.description:
Join PwC Advisory to become part of our Strategy & Transformation Banking Team in Zurich.
Be part of our L&F Team and help our banking clients to transform their liquidity and funding practices to improve their opportunity cost efficiency and to fulfil internal and external requirements, while closely collaborating with Finance, Banking Restructuring and Risk Teams.
BE responsible to further develop the go-to-market strategy for liquidity and funding and manage projects with our key banking clients.
Developi solutions that will help our clients to efficiently measure, manage and use their liquid assets on a short-term operational and mid-term structural basis and integrate that view into their risk understanding in a rising interest rate environment.
Continuously further develop the service offering for the liquidity and funding practice and provide thought leadership to the banking sector.
Be part of a team that operates strongly in an interdisciplinary environment within PwC. You will be using and building bridges to our fellow teams that work at the technological edge in big data analysis and artificial intelligence.
Work in a global environment with the largest banks, you will be embedded in a network of our company that gathers and shares industry insights in Liquidity & Funding worldwide.

An excellent University / ETH Master's degree in Banking, Finance, Economics, Business Administration or an adjacent field that enables you to work with banks and other financial service companies.
A strong affinity for numbers and logical processes, are excellent with Excel and other quantitative programs and feel highly comfortable showing your ideas in Power Point.
Take responsibility for constantly increasing your knowledge in Banking on a personal, intrinsic basis throughout your career.
More than 3 years (SA) / 5 years (M) of hands-on experience in the Banking Sector or Consulting and have gathered extensive experience working on liquidity and funding topics incl. Funds Transfer Pricing (FTP) and working with Trading, Treasury Departments, Financial Risk or Finance and Accounting Departments.
Extensive experience with complex banking products.
Fluent in English. German is a highly significant plus and other languages (e.g. French) are welcome.
Willing to travel to our client's international locations if required but are not disappointed if you are not needed to leave Zurich at all for a given time.
Programming skills are highly recommended and beneficial (e.g. python).
CFA, FRM certificates are a significant plus.

jobs.title: Senior Manager / Director Asset Management – Strategy & Transformation
jobs.description:
Join PwC Advisory to become part of our Financial Services Strategy & Transformation Team in Zurich.
Develop subject matter expertise at the very heart of how Asset Management and portfolio management including alternative investments, and other FS companies works, both on-site with clients and off-site with our international learning and education offerings and industry collaborations.
Advice the Asset Management clients across the full Asset Management value chain and asset classes including portfolio management and the underlying technology platforms.
Support the Asset Management clients to successfully manage their transformations including but not limited to investment management system selection and implementation, definition of target operating models, definition of data models, etc.
Be part of a team that operates strongly in an interdisciplinary environment within PwC. You will be using and building bridges to our fellow teams that work at the technological edge in big data analysis, artificial intelligence and robotic process automation.
Be responsible to lead large scale transformations with our asset management clients and lead business development efforts to generate leads and opportunities.
Work in a global environment with the largest FS companies (banks, insurances, crypto companies, etc.).
Live life-long learning; therefore, we will strongly encourage you to deepen your industry knowledge with adequate certifications.

Excellent University / ETH Master's degree in Banking, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, Information Technology or an adjacent field that enables you to work with asset managers and banks.
Ideally at least 8-10 years of experience in the Asset Management and Portfolio Management sector interacting in a global environment.
Expert in Asset Management, understand the full AM value chain and have multiple year of Asset Management consulting experience.
Experience with the leading investment management systems on the market and have worked with those systems before.
Experienced in leading projects and running command centers for large asset management transformations and post-merger integration.
Strong affinity for numbers and logical processes, are excellent with Excel and other quantitative programs and feel highly comfortable showing your ideas in Power Point.
You work well in a team, and you are able to work independently when needed.
Self-starter and fast learner, and can work well under tight deadlines.
Fluent in German and / or English, other languages are a plus.
Willing to travel to our client's international locations if required but are not disappointed if you are not needed to leave Zurich at all for a given time.

Excellent University / ETH Master's degree in Banking, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, Information Technology or an adjacent field that enables you to work with asset managers and banks.
Ideally at least 8-10 years of experience in the Asset Management and Portfolio Management sector interacting in a global environment.
Expert in Asset Management, understand the full AM value chain and have multiple year of Asset Management consulting experience.
Experience with the leading investment management systems on the market and have worked with those systems before.
Experienced in leading projects and running command centers for large asset management transformations and post-merger integration.
Strong affinity for numbers and logical processes, are excellent with Excel and other quantitative programs and feel highly comfortable showing your ideas in Power Point.
You work well in a team, and you are able to work independently when needed.
Self-starter and fast learner, and can work well under tight deadlines.
Fluent in German and / or English, other languages are a plus.
Willing to travel to our client's international locations if required but are not disappointed if you are not needed to leave Zurich at all for a given time.

jobs.title: Manager Transaction Services - Financial Services
jobs.description:
Helping our clients in the Financial Services industry with dedicated support for transactions in a wide variety of circumstances (acquisitions, divestitures, joint ventures & structured investments).
You will have the opportunity to work across the areas of buy-side due diligence, sell-side assistance and vendor due diligence, deal and negotiation support for both corporate and private equity clients.
Collaborating with other experts to develop a rapid understanding of the client's business and issues, and articulate your findings appropriately in order to deliver high quality reports.
Working in a diversified, international environment covering multiple areas.
Teaming up with a network of highly talented and knowledgeable experts.

You have a bachelor's or master's degree and have a background in finance (CPA or CFA).
You have a thorough understanding of financial accounting and reporting requirements in accordance with internationally recognized reporting standards.
At least 5 years of work experience, including 3 years in providing financial due diligence and other transaction-related services in the Financial Services industry.
Fluent in English, any other language is a plus.
You are energetic, proactive, able to empathise with clients and staff, and are enthusiastic about delivering value to clients.
Flexible and demonstrate willingness to conduct business travel for client assignments or business-related matters.
Excellent analytical skills and are experienced in using Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and additional analytical software tools (e.g. PowerBI, Alteryx) to analyse and present data in a meaningful and efficient way.

jobs.title: Codeur·euse médical·e 60-100%
jobs.description:
Conduisez ou accompagnez les révisions du codage conformément au règlement d'exécution sous SwissDRG.
Assurez le conseil médical et le conseil en gestion d'entreprise dans le cadre du controlling stratégique et opérationnel.
Prenez en charge principalement les clients situés en Suisse romande.
Développez vos compétences techniques et vos "soft skills" grâce à diverses formations internes et externes, dans un environnement favorisant le coaching et le feedback actif.

Diplôme de codage médical avec brevet fédéral et plusieurs années d'expérience dans le domaine, qualification pour maintenir la liste des réviseurs de l'OFS.
Formation médicale et solides connaissances des tarifs.
Excellente maîtrise orale et écrite du français, ainsi que de bonnes connaissances en allemand ou en anglais.
Excellentes aptitudes en communication, fort engagement, orientation client et esprit d'équipe.
Méthode de travail autonome et rigoureuse.

jobs.title: Steuerspezialist:in mit Fokus auf Steuerdeklarationen
jobs.description:
Erstelle und überprüfe Gewinnsteuererklärungen und Steuerveranlagungen für juristische Personen.
Begleite die Abschlussplanung von Unternehmen und berechne dabei die erforderlichen Steuerrückstellungen.
Bearbeite steuerliche Anfragen.
Unterstütze bei Unternehmensstrukturierungen, Transaktionen und Steuerplanungsprojekten.
Erwerbe umfassendes technisches Know-how und Soft Skills durch On-and-off-the-job-Trainings.
Arbeite in einem inspirierenden Team und schärfe dein persönliches und berufliches Profil durch regelmässiges Feedback von Kolleg: innen und Vorgesetzten.

Habe eine Ausbildung im Bereich Steuern oder eine abgeschlossene kaufmännische Grundausbildung mit Erfahrung oder suche den Wiedereinstieg im Steuerbereich für juristische Personen.
Sei eine hoch motivierte, dynamische und zielstrebige Persönlichkeit, die analytisches Arbeiten schätzt und sich nicht vor komplexen Fragestellungen scheut.
Zeige hohe Lernbereitschaft und habe ein ausgeprägtes Flair für Zahlen.
Übernehme von Anfang an Verantwortung und bewege dich in einem Umfeld mit aktiver Coaching- und Feedbackkultur.
Weise ausgezeichnete EDV-Kenntnisse in Word, Excel und PowerPoint auf.
Sei stilsicher in Deutsch und habe sehr gute Englischkenntnisse.

jobs.title: Tax Accounting Senior Associate
jobs.description:
Work in our growing tax accounting team in Zurich. Occasional deployment at other locations in Switzerland is possible.
Support our customers in tax accounting matters with regard to various accounting standards (e.g. IFRS, US GAAP, etc.). This includes providing support in our client's provision processes as well as supporting assurance teams with procedures to be performed over income tax related positions.
Support engagement teams with Pillar 2 related questions including calculations and related technical assessments
Work on external publications and support the development of internal training materials, newsletters, etc.
Work in a dynamic team in an international environment where there is a lot to learn and opportunities to develop.

Ready for a challenge in a new, dynamic and interesting environment with have a flair for accounting and taxes.
Bachelor's or similar degree in economics, Finance, Accounting or similar field and preferably already first (internship) experiences in auditing / financial accounting / tax consulting.
Fluent in English, any additional language is an asset
Able to take on responsibility right from the start and move confidently in a dynamic, demanding environment.
Motivated, resilient and determined personality who is not afraid of complex issues. You can deal with change and adapt quickly to new/spontaneous circumstances.

jobs.title: Manager and Senior Manager in Life Sciences R&D Management Consulting
jobs.description:
As a Manager / Senior Manager in our fast growing Life Sciences R&D Consulting team, you will be at the heart of transforming how leading pharma, biotech and medtech organisations discover, develop and deliver therapies to patients. You will:Sell, lead and deliver high impact projects – Shape and close new opportunities, then lead diverse teams to deliver complex programmes that improve R&D productivity, accelerate development timelines, enhance quality and compliance, and unlock new value for our clients.
Develop new ideas and propositions – Identify emerging client needs and industry trends, and turn them into distinctive consulting offerings, thought leadership and market leading solutions that you will take directly to clients.
Work cross functionally across the value chain – Solve end to end challenges spanning R&D, manufacturing and commercial, helping clients break down silos and design integrated operating models, processes and technologies. Collaborate across borders and disciplines – Work with international clients and teams, partnering closely with PwC's global network of life sciences, digital, data/AI and change specialists to bring the best of PwC to every engagement.
Shape a high growth practice – Play a key role in building our rapidly expanding team: developing our people, strengthening our culture, and helping to define how we work and what we take to market as we scale.
You'll work on mission critical issues for top tier life sciences companies, while contributing directly to the growth and direction of a dynamic and entrepreneurial practice within PwC.

Bachelor's or Master's degree in a science related discipline (e.g. life sciences, chemistry, biomedical engineering, pharmacy or related field)
At least 6 years' relevant work experience, with a strong track record of delivering results in complex environments
Minimum 3 years' experience in the consulting industry (mandatory), ideally with exposure to life sciences / healthcare clients
Fluency in English (written and spoken); additional knowledge of German and/or French is highly desirable
Willingness and ability to travel to serve international clients as needed
Experience utilizing AI in both client delivery and as consulting tools

What gives you a competitive edge:
Experience working with a start up or entrepreneurial mentality – proactive, hands on, and comfortable building from a blank sheet
High comfort with ambiguity and change, with the ability to adapt quickly and remain effective in uncertain or evolving situations
Proven ability to manage complexity – across multi workstream projects, diverse stakeholder groups and global organisations
Agile and intellectually curious mindset, continuously seeking new ways to solve problems, learn new topics and apply innovative approaches
Good understanding of the clinical development lifecycle and associated functions, such as:
* Portfolio and scenario planning
* Regulatory strategy and submissions
* Pre commercialisation activities (e.g. market access, medical affairs, launch readiness)
* Commercialisation activities (e.g. go to market strategy, brand planning, lifecycle management)

Demonstrated experience in sales and business development, including identifying opportunities, contributing to proposals and pitches, and building long term client relationships

Willingness and ability to travel to serve international clients as needed
Experience utilizing AI in both client delivery and as consulting tools

What gives you a competitive edge:
Experience working with a start up or entrepreneurial mentality – proactive, hands on, and comfortable building from a blank sheet
High comfort with ambiguity and change, with the ability to adapt quickly and remain effective in uncertain or evolving situations
Proven ability to manage complexity – across multi workstream projects, diverse stakeholder groups and global organisations
Agile and intellectually curious mindset, continuously seeking new ways to solve problems, learn new topics and apply innovative approaches
Good understanding of the clinical development lifecycle and associated functions, such as:
* Portfolio and scenario planning
* Regulatory strategy and submissions
* Pre commercialisation activities (e.g. market access, medical affairs, launch readiness)
* Commercialisation activities (e.g. go to market strategy, brand planning, lifecycle management)

Demonstrated experience in sales and business development, including identifying opportunities, contributing to proposals and pitches, and building long term client relationships
descriptionByLocale.en:
As a Manager / Senior Manager in our fast growing Life Sciences R&D Consulting team, you will be at the heart of transforming how leading pharma, biotech and medtech organisations discover, develop and deliver therapies to patients. You will:Sell, lead and deliver high impact projects – Shape and close new opportunities, then lead diverse teams to deliver complex programmes that improve R&D productivity, accelerate development timelines, enhance quality and compliance, and unlock new value for our clients.
Develop new ideas and propositions – Identify emerging client needs and industry trends, and turn them into distinctive consulting offerings, thought leadership and market leading solutions that you will take directly to clients.
Work cross functionally across the value chain – Solve end to end challenges spanning R&D, manufacturing and commercial, helping clients break down silos and design integrated operating models, processes and technologies. Collaborate across borders and disciplines – Work with international clients and teams, partnering closely with PwC's global network of life sciences, digital, data/AI and change specialists to bring the best of PwC to every engagement.
Shape a high growth practice – Play a key role in building our rapidly expanding team: developing our people, strengthening our culture, and helping to define how we work and what we take to market as we scale.
You'll work on mission critical issues for top tier life sciences companies, while contributing directly to the growth and direction of a dynamic and entrepreneurial practice within PwC.

Bachelor's or Master's degree in a science related discipline (e.g. life sciences, chemistry, biomedical engineering, pharmacy or related field)
At least 6 years' relevant work experience, with a strong track record of delivering results in complex environments

jobs.title: Cybersecurity & Privacy Senior Associate
jobs.description:
This is an exciting opportunity to join our Cybersecurity & Privacy team, a multidisciplinary group of consultants, engineers, and risk specialists who help clients build digital trust, manage cyber risk, and comply with evolving regulations.

You will benefit from PwC's global network, proven methodologies, and a culture that values inclusion, continuous learning, and practical innovation.

Expect a collaborative environment where you can take ownership of workstreams, coach junior team members, and contribute to thought leadership while developing your expertise through engagements, training, and certifications.

Deliver client engagements focused on cyber risk and privacy, including assessments, gap analyses, roadmaps, and implementation projects.
Translate technical and regulatory requirements into pragmatic policies, standards, architectures, and operating procedures tailored to the client's risk appetite.
Design and implement security controls aligned to frameworks such as ISO/IEC 27001, NIST CSF, CIS Controls, and industry regulations (e.g., FINMA circulars, DORA, NIS2).
Support clients in maturing capabilities across cloud security, identity and access management, data security (encryption, DLP), vulnerability and threat management, security monitoring, and incident response.
Assess third-party and supply chain cyber risk, define remediation plans, and help establish continuous assurance and vendor oversight processes.
Advise on privacy compliance and operations (e.g., GDPR, Swiss nFADP), including data protection impact assessments, records of processing activities, data subject rights, cross border transfers, and privacy-by-design.
Lead workstreams, manage stakeholders, and ensure high-quality deliverables: reports, playbooks, risk registers, control matrices, and board-ready presentations.
Contribute to business development: shape proposals, respond to RFPs, build repeatable assets, and develop points of view on emerging topics.
Coach and mentor junior colleagues, foster a culture of teaming and excellence, and stay current with threat trends and regulatory developments.

2–5 years of experience in cybersecurity and/or privacy consulting or a similar role (internal or external), with proven delivery of client-facing projects.- Strong knowledge of key frameworks and regulations: ISO/IEC 27001/2, NIST CSF, CIS Controls; awareness of NIS2, CRA, DORA, GDPR, Swiss nFADP and sector-specific guidance (e.g., FINMA) is a plus.
Practical experience in several of the following: security governance and risk; privacy operations; identity and access management; data security; compliance with cybersecurity and resilience regulations; threat and vulnerability management; security operations and incident response; cloud security.
Consulting core skills: structured problem-solving, clear writing, confident presentation, stakeholder management, and the ability to translate complex topics into actionable recommendations.
Education in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Information Security, Engineering, Law, Business) or equivalent practical experience.
Professional certifications are advantageous (e.g., SSCP, Security+).
Language skills: fluent in English; French is a strong asset.

jobs.title: AI & Data Consultant – Emerging Technologies
jobs.description:
Shape the future of AI transformation

We are a team of technology experts and enthusiasts dedicated to transforming and disrupting. Our mission is to design, build, and scale AI solutions that redefine how legal, compliance, and tax services are delivered. Combining deep technical expertise in machine learning and artificial intelligence with a sharp focus on market needs, we transform research insights into innovative, practical solutions that deliver real-world impact.

Act as a trusted advisor to clients from strategy to operations, solving complex challenges with modern data stacks, cloud-enabled platforms, and machine learning/AI techniques.
Form and lead multidisciplinary engagement teams that combine business, technology, and industry expertise to deliver tailored, end-to-end solutions.
Collaborate with domain experts to drive digital transformation by automating processes, unlocking insights from documents and transactions, and building AI-powered tools that reshape how tax and legal services are delivered.
Work with clients to define data and AI strategies, clarify technology needs, and craft compelling, technically grounded visions for their future data and AI landscape.
Modernise clients' data environments using state-of-the-art architectures and best-practice implementation approaches and develop self-service analytics solutions with intuitive data and model visualisations that drive actionable insights.
Discover and shape business development opportunities based on assessments of the current technology landscape and emerging trends, acting as a “data influencer” who drives continuous improvement and adoption of modern technologies within PwC.
Contribute to and learn from a diverse, international team (85+ nationalities) working on projects in data analytics, machine learning, generative AI, and business intelligence for national and international clients across financial services, life sciences, consumer goods and other industries.

Master's degree or PhD in Computer or Natural Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related technical field; business education is a plus.
3+ years' experience delivering or implementing AI/data solutions in consulting, product, or research environments, with proven hands-on work in LLMs, machine learning, data engineering, or AI solution development.
Expertise in at least one of: generative and non-generative AI, legal tech, tax tech, data quality and data management, or BI/analytics engineering, ideally including exposure to legal/tax topics such as legal processes, knowledge management and retrieval, risk evaluation, or compliance workflows.
Proficiency in Python or a similar programming/scripting language (e.g. TypeScript, JavaScript, Bash).
Strong business acumen, with the ability to identify business development opportunities and design creative solutions using data and technology, plus prior experience in consulting or managing client/customer-facing relationships.
Excellent communication skills, able to articulate complex technological concepts and data insights to business stakeholders; strong team player and exceptional problem solver with a client-focused mindset and desire to keep learning.
Fluency in German and English.

Shape the future of AI transformation

We are a team of technology experts and enthusiasts dedicated to transforming and disrupting. Our mission is to design, build, and scale AI solutions that redefine how legal, compliance, and tax services are delivered. Combining deep technical expertise in machine learning and artificial intelligence with a sharp focus on market needs, we transform research insights into innovative, practical solutions that deliver real-world impact.

Act as a trusted advisor to clients from strategy to operations, solving complex challenges with modern data stacks, cloud-enabled platforms, and machine learning/AI techniques.
Form and lead multidisciplinary engagement teams that combine business, technology, and industry expertise to deliver tailored, end-to-end solutions.
Collaborate with domain experts to drive digital transformation by automating processes, unlocking insights from documents and transactions, and building AI-powered tools that reshape how tax and legal services are delivered.
Work with clients to define data and AI strategies, clarify technology needs, and craft compelling, technically grounded visions for their future data and AI landscape.
Modernise clients' data environments using state-of-the-art architectures and best-practice implementation approaches and develop self-service analytics solutions with intuitive data and model visualisations that drive actionable insights.
Discover and shape business development opportunities based on assessments of the current technology landscape and emerging trends, acting as a “data influencer” who drives continuous improvement and adoption of modern technologies within PwC.
Contribute to and learn from a diverse, international team (85+ nationalities) working on projects in data analytics, machine learning, generative AI, and business intelligence for national and international clients across financial services, life sciences, consumer goods and other industries.

Master's degree or PhD in Computer or Natural Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related technical field; business education is a plus.
3+ years' experience delivering or implementing AI/data solutions in consulting, product, or research environments, with proven hands-on work in LLMs, machine learning, data engineering, or AI solution development.
Expertise in at least one of: generative and non-generative AI, legal tech, tax tech, data quality and data management, or BI/analytics engineering, ideally including exposure to legal/tax topics such as legal processes, knowledge management and retrieval, risk evaluation, or compliance workflows.
Proficiency in Python or a similar programming/scripting language (e.g. TypeScript, JavaScript, Bash).
Strong business acumen, with the ability to identify business development opportunities and design creative solutions using data and technology, plus prior experience in consulting or managing client/customer-facing relationships.
Excellent communication skills, able to articulate complex technological concepts and data insights to business stakeholders; strong team player and exceptional problem solver with a client-focused mindset and desire to keep learning.
Fluency in German and English.

jobs.title: AI & Business Transformation Senior Manager
jobs.description:
Do you want to shape how AI and data transform the way organisations work? Are you ready to lead end-to-end AI and data solutions that change how clients deliver their services?

You will join a diverse, international team of AI, data, and subject matter specialists who work closely together, learn from each other, and bring ideas to market quickly. As a Senior Manager, you set the direction for engagements, take a leading role in client relationships, and help grow our AI and business transformation offering.

You work directly with senior stakeholders on AI and data strategies, define architectures and solution blueprints, and ensure solutions are robust, scalable, and adopted in practice.

Shape AI and data strategies with clients, defining vision, target architectures, and roadmaps for AI‑enabled business and workforce transformations, and translating strategic priorities into concrete programmes, offerings, and go‑to‑market initiatives.
Manage end‑to‑end AI, ML, and data solution design and delivery, from problem framing and discovery to production rollout and adoption, with a proven track record of moving use cases from prototype to production.
Architect and oversee implementation of modern data and AI platforms (e.g. cloud‑based data stacks, MLOps practices, integration with existing systems and processes), ensuring robust, scalable, and secure solutions.
Design and supervise development of AI and GenAI use cases, such as document intelligence, knowledge retrieval, workflow automation, risk and compliance models, and AI assistants for professionals, turning pilots and proofs of concept into scalable offerings and reusable assets.
Act as a thought leader and innovation driver: scan emerging AI technologies, identify new opportunities, and help build our market presence and pipeline through differentiated AI solutions.
Take a leading role in client conversations, workshops, and executive discussions, ensuring clear communication, strong stakeholder alignment, and trusted relationships with senior client stakeholders.
Lead multidisciplinary engagement teams, coach and mentor junior colleagues, and provide organisational and delivery leadership, including solution architecture governance, change enablement, and internal process excellence.

Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, or a related technical field; additional business education (e.g. MBA, executive programmes) is a plus.
Extensive experience (typically 8+ years) in designing and delivering AI/ML and data solutions, including at least 3–4 years in management consulting, technology consulting, or a comparable client-facing advisory role; additional experience in product or complex enterprise environments is an advantage.
Deep hands-on expertise in machine learning and AI, including generative AI and large language models, plus solid experience in at least one of: document and knowledge management, legal or tax technology, risk and compliance analytics, or workflow automation.
Strong technical toolkit: proficiency in Python and common ML/AI frameworks; experience with modern data and ML platforms (e.g. Azure, AWS or GCP; Databricks; Snowflake; MLflow; vector databases; MLOps toolchains).
Proven experience leading multidisciplinary teams and complex projects, including solution architecture, delivery governance, stakeholder management, and change enablement.
Strong organisational and operational leadership skills, with the ability to drive structure, run essential internal processes, and ensure delivery excellence.
Excellent communication skills in German and English, with the ability to explain complex AI and data topics to both technical and non-technical audiences and to facilitate workshops, steering committees, and executive discussions.

jobs.title: Associate / Senior Associate – Deals – Separation and Integration
jobs.description:
Als Mitglied des Deals Strategy und Operations Teams konzentrierst Du Dich darauf, erfolgreiche M&A Transaktionen zu planen und umzusetzen. Wir unterstützen unsere Kunden:innen bei komplexen und international ausgerichteten M&A-Projekten. Du wirst Teil unseres wachsenden Deals-Teams, das in der Schweiz die führende Position einnimmt. Das Deals-Team bietet unseren Kunden:innen ein breites Spektrum an M&A-Beratungsdienstleistungen von der Entwicklung der Transaktionsstrategie bis zu ihrer Umsetzung an. Weitere Informationen findest du hier

Stell sicher, dass Transaktionen operativ funktionieren, Risiken in komplexen M&A-Deals reduziert werden und das geplante Wertschöpfungspotenzial tatsächlich realisiert wird.
Unterstütze anspruchsvolle Beratungsprojekte für wichtige Branchenakteure. Baue vertrauensvolle Beziehungen zu internen und externen Partnern:innen auf und pflege sie kontinuierlich.
Führe Analysen zur Bereitschaft für Integration und Separationen (Carve-out) durch und unterstütze Kunden bei der Vorbereitung für Day-1 und Day-100.
Unterstütze Separations- und Integrationsprojekte mit hoher Komplexität, in einer multinationalen Umgebung.
Entwerfe und implementiere Operating Models und entwickle Separations- oder Integrationspläne, die sicherstellen, dass das volle Wertschöpfungspotenzial von Transaktionen realisiert wird.
Trage aktiv zum Wachstum unseres Deals Strategy & Operations Teams bei, indem du deinen Unternehmergeist und dein persönliches Netzwerk nutzt.
Werde Teil eines positiven, sich gegenseitig unterstützenden Teams mit flachen Hierarchien, das dir vielfältige berufliche Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten bietet. Wir bieten dir dabei strukturierte Karrierepfade mit Coaching durch erfahrene Deals-Kollegen:innen, regelmässige Trainings, sowie die Möglichkeit, früh Verantwortung für Workstreams zu übernehmen.

Master-Abschluss (vergleichbare Abschlüsse sind erwünscht) und mindestens 1-5 Jahre relevante Berufserfahrung in M&A- und/oder Beratungsprojekten bei einer renommierten Beratungsfirma oder Big4. Alternativ Erfahrung aus internem M&A-Team oder transformativen M&A Projekt einer größeren Unternehmung.
Ausgeprägte Projektmanagementfähigkeiten und Erfahrung entlang des M&A-Lebenszyklus, in Separations- und/oder Integrationsprojekten.
Gutes Verständnis von spezifischen Geschäftsfunktionen im Deals-Umfeld (z.B. Marketing & Sales, Supply Chain, IT).
Idealerweise Erfahrung in verschiedenen der folgenden Bereiche: Mitarbeit an einem Separations-/Integrationsprgroamm, Erfahrung in der Planung und Durchführung von Integrationen und Separationen, Erstellung von TSAs (inkl. Preisgestaltung), Vorbereitung von TSA-Exitplänen und Management des TSA-Exits (inkl. TSA-Exit-Costing), Sicherstellung der Day-1-Readiness, Operating Model Design und Implementierung, Leitung eines PMOs (IMO oder SMO)
Du fühlst dich wohl dabei, Kunden:innen aus einer Vielzahl von Branchen zu beraten. Erfahrung in einer bestimmten Branche ist von Vorteil.
Du fühlst du dich wohl beim Interpretieren von Finanzberichten, verfügst über hervorragende analytische und kommunikative Fähigkeiten und sprichst fliessend Englisch und Deutsch. Jede weitere Sprache ist von Vorteil.
Du bist bereit zu reisen, um die Projekte für unsere international aufgestellten Kunden:innen umzusetzen.

Entwerfe und implementiere Operating Models und entwickle Separations- oder Integrationspläne, die sicherstellen, dass das volle Wertschöpfungspotenzial von Transaktionen realisiert wird.
Trage aktiv zum Wachstum unseres Deals Strategy & Operations Teams bei, indem du deinen Unternehmergeist und dein persönliches Netzwerk nutzt.
Werde Teil eines positiven, sich gegenseitig unterstützenden Teams mit flachen Hierarchien, das dir vielfältige berufliche Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten bietet. Wir bieten dir dabei strukturierte Karrierepfade mit Coaching durch erfahrene Deals-Kollegen:innen, regelmässige Trainings, sowie die Möglichkeit, früh Verantwortung für Workstreams zu übernehmen.

Master-Abschluss (vergleichbare Abschlüsse sind erwünscht) und mindestens 1-5 Jahre relevante Berufserfahrung in M&A- und/oder Beratungsprojekten bei einer renommierten Beratungsfirma oder Big4. Alternativ Erfahrung aus internem M&A-Team oder transformativen M&A Projekt einer größeren Unternehmung.
Ausgeprägte Projektmanagementfähigkeiten und Erfahrung entlang des M&A-Lebenszyklus, in Separations- und/oder Integrationsprojekten.
Gutes Verständnis von spezifischen Geschäftsfunktionen im Deals-Umfeld (z.B. Marketing & Sales, Supply Chain, IT).
Idealerweise Erfahrung in verschiedenen der folgenden Bereiche: Mitarbeit an einem Separations-/Integrationsprgroamm, Erfahrung in der Planung und Durchführung von Integrationen und Separationen, Erstellung von TSAs (inkl. Preisgestaltung), Vorbereitung von TSA-Exitplänen und Management des TSA-Exits (inkl. TSA-Exit-Costing), Sicherstellung der Day-1-Readiness, Operating Model Design und Implementierung, Leitung eines PMOs (IMO oder SMO)
Du fühlst dich wohl dabei, Kunden:innen aus einer Vielzahl von Branchen zu beraten. Erfahrung in einer bestimmten Branche ist von Vorteil.
Du fühlst du dich wohl beim Interpretieren von Finanzberichten, verfügst über hervorragende analytische und kommunikative Fähigkeiten und sprichst fliessend Englisch und Deutsch. Jede weitere Sprache ist von Vorteil.
Du bist bereit zu reisen, um die Projekte für unsere international aufgestellten Kunden:innen umzusetzen.

jobs.title: Manager/Senior Manager in SAP Group Reporting
jobs.description:
Work in a challenging, international and fast paced SAP transformation programs focusing on design and implementation of Group Consolidation solutions for financial consolidation enabled by SAP and S/4HANA.
Analyze our client's business finance and controlling processes, gather the requirements and provide best in class advice on design and implementation of consolidation solutions.
Manage sub-projects and lead teams of functional consultants and client staff. 
Acuire a broad range of technical and soft skills through on the job and off the job training. 
Be part of a growing, dynamic team blending SAP innovation and industry expertise.

A degree (bachelor / master / diploma) preferred in business, IT, engineering or business information systems or digital discipline. Preferably focused and specialized on finance/controlling during your primarily and further education. 
5 years of experience in process consulting, design and implementation of SAP group consolidation solutions with focus on SAP S/4 HANA Group Reporting.  Experience with other SAP consolidation solutions SEM-BCS, EC-CS or BPC is beneficial.
Expert knowledge in group closing and financial consolidation topics and a very good understanding regarding the overall finance integration across finance and operations.  
Profound experience in setting up balance carry forward, currency translation, intercompany elimination, consolidation of investments and Validation processes.
Strong analytical skills with a “can do” attitude, fluent in English and German. Any other languages are a plus.
Flexible to work on engagements in Switzerland and/or abroad.

jobs.title: Asset Management Audit - Manager
jobs.description:
Join our Asset Management Audit team at PwC and be part of a group that goes beyond auditing financial statements. We help asset managers, funds, and investment firms navigate evolving regulations, assess risks, and optimize business processes. By leveraging cutting-edge technologies, data-driven insights, and a diverse team, we drive innovation and build trust in the financial world. Every project is unique, offering you the chance to contribute your expertise, gain new perspectives, and make a real impact. Together, we shape a sustainable, innovative, and global future

Manage financial and regulatory audits of asset managers, management companies and traditional, real estate and alternative funds.
Actively participate in defining the audit approach and ensure that the audit is completed in full compliance with auditing standards and methodology.
Play a key role in leading, supporting, and coaching your teams to achieve collective goals and foster continuous professional development.
Prepare audit files for Partner review and assist in presenting audit findings to management or Boards of Directors.
Develop and strengthen relationships with clients, providing expert advice on technical accounting and regulatory matters.
Stay ahead of regulatory developments and help clients adapt to new requirements, including ESG reporting and other emerging trends.

Swiss Certified Public Accountant qualification or an equivalent professional certification (e.g., ACCA, ACA, CPA).
At least 5 years of professional experience in external audits of entities and vehicles of the Asset Management industry..
A passion for fostering a collaborative coaching culture, with a strong ability to mentor and support less experienced colleagues while sharing knowledge.
A strong affinity for IT and digital tools, with hands-on experience using Big Data tools such as Alteryx or Power BI to drive digital transformation and enhance audit processes.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in French; proficiency in English.
A proactive and innovative mindset, with the ability to adapt to a fast-changing regulatory and technological environment.

jobs.title: Digital Audit Manager (Financial Services)
jobs.description:
Stell dir vor, du bist Teil eines Teams, das Unternehmen dabei unterstützt, ihre IT- und Geschäftsprozesse zukunftssicher zu gestalten. Bei PwC im Bereich Digital Audit-Financial Services arbeitest du mit Expert:innen zusammen, die mit Leidenschaft und Innovationsgeist die digitale Transformation der Finanzwelt vorantreiben.

Ob Banken, Versicherungen oder Asset Manager-wir analysieren IT-gestützte Prozesse, identifizieren Risiken und entwickeln innovative Lösungen, die Vertrauen schaffen. Dabei setzen wir auf modernste Technologien, datenbasierte Ansätze und ein starkes Team, das Vielfalt lebt und voneinander lernt.

Führe anspruchsvolle Digital-Audit-Projekte durch, insbesondere im Bereich IT General Controls (ITGCs), und unterstütze bei der Prüfung von ERP-Systemen und Geschäftsprozessen.
Identifiziere Schwachstellen in IT-Kontrollsystemen, analysiere Risiken und entwickle massgeschneiderte Empfehlungen zur Optimierung.
Entwickle innovative Lösungen, die auf die individuellen Bedürfnisse und Herausforderungen unserer Kunden eingehen.
Baue starke Kundenbeziehungen auf, präsentiere überzeugend und gewinne neue Projekte, um unsere Marktposition weiter zu stärken.
Leite und motiviere Teams in einem dynamischen Umfeld, fördere die Entwicklung deiner Kolleg:innen und teile dein Wissen.
Nutze modernste Technologien wie Datenanalysen, Automatisierung und KI, um unsere Prüfungsprozesse effizienter und zukunftsorientierter zu gestalten.

Bachelor- oder Masterabschluss in Information System Management, Management oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang.
CISA- oder CISM-Zertifizierung.
5-6 Jahre Erfahrung in den Bereichen IT-Audit, IT-Consulting oder der Prüfung von IT-gestützten Geschäftsprozessen bei einer Big4 Firma.
Nachweisbare Erfolge im Business Development sowie in der Leitung von Angeboten und Pitches.
Ausgeprägte Fähigkeiten im Projektmanagement und in der Führung und Entwicklung von Teams.
Hervorragende Kommunikations- und Präsentationsfähigkeiten sowie Erfahrung im Verfassen präziser Berichte auf Deutsch und Englisch - Französisch von Vorteil.
Fähigkeit, eigenständig und ohne tägliche Anleitung zu arbeiten, gepaart mit stark ausgeprägten Networking- und Beziehungsmanagement-Kompetenzen.
Fundierte Kenntnisse in mindestens einem der folgenden Bereiche:
Prüfung von KI-/ML-Modellen oder AI Governance-oder die Bereitschaft, sich in diese zukunftsweisenden Themen einzuarbeiten
Cloud-Technologien und Cloud Security (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
Cybersecurity und IT-Risikomanagement
Finance transformation
Data Analytics und Data Governance
Robotic Process Automation (RPA) und intelligente Automatisierung
Blockchain-Technologien und Digital Assets

Nachweisbare Erfolge im Business Development sowie in der Leitung von Angeboten und Pitches.
Ausgeprägte Fähigkeiten im Projektmanagement und in der Führung und Entwicklung von Teams.
Hervorragende Kommunikations- und Präsentationsfähigkeiten sowie Erfahrung im Verfassen präziser Berichte auf Deutsch und Englisch - Französisch von Vorteil.
Fähigkeit, eigenständig und ohne tägliche Anleitung zu arbeiten, gepaart mit stark ausgeprägten Networking- und Beziehungsmanagement-Kompetenzen.
Fundierte Kenntnisse in mindestens einem der folgenden Bereiche:
Prüfung von KI-/ML-Modellen oder AI Governance-oder die Bereitschaft, sich in diese zukunftsweisenden Themen einzuarbeiten
Cloud-Technologien und Cloud Security (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
Cybersecurity und IT-Risikomanagement
Finance transformation
Data Analytics und Data Governance
Robotic Process Automation (RPA) und intelligente Automatisierung
Blockchain-Technologien und Digital Assets
descriptionByLocale.de:
Stell dir vor, du bist Teil eines Teams, das Unternehmen dabei unterstützt, ihre IT- und Geschäftsprozesse zukunftssicher zu gestalten. Bei PwC im Bereich Digital Audit-Financial Services arbeitest du mit Expert:innen zusammen, die mit Leidenschaft und Innovationsgeist die digitale Transformation der Finanzwelt vorantreiben.

Ob Banken, Versicherungen oder Asset Manager-wir analysieren IT-gestützte Prozesse, identifizieren Risiken und entwickeln innovative Lösungen, die Vertrauen schaffen. Dabei setzen wir auf modernste Technologien, datenbasierte Ansätze und ein starkes Team, das Vielfalt lebt und voneinander lernt.

Führe anspruchsvolle Digital-Audit-Projekte durch, insbesondere im Bereich IT General Controls (ITGCs), und unterstütze bei der Prüfung von ERP-Systemen und Geschäftsprozessen.
Identifiziere Schwachstellen in IT-Kontrollsystemen, analysiere Risiken und entwickle massgeschneiderte Empfehlungen zur Optimierung.
Entwickle innovative Lösungen, die auf die individuellen Bedürfnisse und Herausforderungen unserer Kunden eingehen.
Baue starke Kundenbeziehungen auf, präsentiere überzeugend und gewinne neue Projekte, um unsere Marktposition weiter zu stärken.
Leite und motiviere Teams in einem dynamischen Umfeld, fördere die Entwicklung deiner Kolleg:innen und teile dein Wissen.
Nutze modernste Technologien wie Datenanalysen, Automatisierung und KI, um unsere Prüfungsprozesse effizienter und zukunftsorientierter zu gestalten.

Bachelor- oder Masterabschluss in Information System Management, Management oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang.
CISA- oder CISM-Zertifizierung.
5-6 Jahre Erfahrung in den Bereichen IT-Audit, IT-Consulting oder der Prüfung von IT-gestützten Geschäftsprozessen bei einer Big4 Firma.
Nachweisbare Erfolge im Business Development sowie in der Leitung von Angeboten und Pitches.
Ausgeprägte Fähigkeiten im Projektmanagement und in der Führung und Entwicklung von Teams.
Hervorragende Kommunikations- und Präsentationsfähigkeiten sowie Erfahrung im Verfassen präziser Berichte auf Deutsch und Englisch - Französisch von Vorteil.
Fähigkeit, eigenständig und ohne tägliche Anleitung zu arbeiten, gepaart mit stark ausgeprägten Networking- und Beziehungsmanagement-Kompetenzen.

jobs.title: Corporate Tax Manager Zentralschweiz
jobs.description:
Du berätst international tätige Schweizer und ausländische Unternehmen in Steuerbelangen und erarbeitest massgeschneiderte Lösungsvorschläge für ihre komplexen Problemstellungen.
Du bist Teil eines inspirierenden Teams, das anspruchsvolle Projekte betreut wie nationale und internationale Unternehmensumstrukturierungen, Transaktionen, Steuerplanungsprojekte und Tax Accounting-Mandate.
Du entwickelst deine eigenen Führungsqualitäten in einem dynamischen Team mit engagierten Mitarbeitern.
Du kannst dich in einem Arbeitsumfeld, das von Teamgeist, Vertrauen und gegenseitiger Unterstützung geprägt ist, voll entfalten.
Du arbeitest an internen und externen Publikationen und Schulungen mit und unterstützt bei fachtechnischen Anlässen.
Du erwirbst umfassendes technisches Know-how und Soft Skills durch On-and-off-the-Job-Trainings.

Du hast einen Hochschulabschluss (Uni oder FH) in Betriebswirtschaft oder Recht o.Ä. und vorzugsweise das Schweizer Steuerexperten-Diplom.
Du besitzt mindestens 4 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Unternehmenssteuerrecht in der Beratung oder der Industrie, allenfalls auch im Bereich Tax Accounting (IFRS oder US GAAP).
Du verfügst über sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift; zusätzliche Sprachkenntnisse sind ein Plus.
Du übernimmst die Führung deines Teams und die Verantwortung für deinen Fachbereich und deine Projekte.
Du bist engagiert, flexibel sowie eine team- und kundenorientierte Persönlichkeit mit ausgezeichneten Kommunikationsfähigkeiten.
Du siehst die grösseren Zusammenhänge und stellst deinen Unternehmergeist und dein Können im Bereich Geschäftsentwicklung gerne unter Beweis.

jobs.title: Senior Associate - Regulatory Risk and Compliance
jobs.description:
Une carrière dans les services de réglementation, de gestion des risques et de conformité vous offrira l'opportunité d'aider les entreprises à repenser leur approche du risque et à créer un avantage en matière de risque durable. Nous faisons partie d'une proposition client unique, aidant nos clients à développer des contrôles internes appropriés en utilisant l'analyse et des solutions technologiques pour soutenir l'exécution efficace de la gouvernance, afin d'optimiser leurs politiques et processus de risque et de conformité, et d'améliorer la performance de l'entreprise.

Vérifier le respect des réglementations financières (gouvernance d'entreprise, règles d'organisation interne, LBA et règles de conduite) dans le domaine bancaire, du négoce de valeurs mobilières et des placements collectifs de capitaux.
Aider et conseiller les intermédiaires financiers dans le cadre des procédures d'autorisation auprès des autorités de surveillance ou des organismes d'autorégulation.
Diriger des missions spéciales visant à déterminer l'existence d'un assujettissement ou à remédier à des situations de violations de la réglementation en vigueur.
Acquérir des connaissances approfondies d'un large éventail de clients.
Rejoindre une équipe active et diversifiée dans un secteur en plein développement.

Détenir un Master en droit des affaires, la possession d'un diplôme en compliance management et/ou du brevet d'avocat est un atout.
3 ans d'expérience dans le domaine bancaire ou financier, une expérience en Big Four un plus.
Parler couramment le français et avoir une bonne maîtrise de l'anglais.
Montrer une capacité à innover, aimer travailler en équipe et être en contact avec les clients.
Posséder d'excellentes capacités de synthèse et d'analyse, ainsi qu'un bon sens de la négociation.
Résider en Suisse.

jobs.title: U.S. Tax Manager – Private Clients
jobs.description:
We are seeking a highly skilled U.S. Tax Manager to lead and manage complex tax engagements for our private clients. In this role, you will leverage your comprehensive understanding of U.S. tax regulations and financial planning to provide strategic tax planning and compliance services tailored to high-net-worth individuals. Your ability to interpret complex tax data will be essential in navigating the ever-evolving tax landscape for our clients.

In this role, you will:
Build and maintain strong relationships with private clients, understanding their unique financial needs and delivering tailored tax solutions.
Develop and implement tax planning strategies that align with client objectives and industry standards, maximizing tax efficiencies for high-net-worth individuals.
Oversee the preparation and review of complex tax returns, ensuring compliance with U.S. tax regulations and internal standards.
Provide expert guidance on complex tax issues, leveraging your in-depth knowledge of U.S. tax regulations and their application to private client scenarios.
Interpret and analyze tax data to generate insightful reports and recommendations, assisting clients in making informed financial decisions.

A minimum of 5 years of U.S. taxation experience with an international accounting firm or similar, specializing in U.S. compliance and consulting tax issues for high-net-worth individuals.
A bachelor's degree with a specialization in accounting/taxation or a law degree from an accredited university.
CPA or IRS Enrolled Agent qualification.
Extensive technical skills relevant to private clients, coupled with a successful track record in identifying client needs and pursuing new service opportunities.
Proven leadership experience, including establishing direction, motivating team members, and fostering innovation.
Demonstrated success in generating new business, writing proposals, and collaborating across engagements and business units.
Advanced knowledge of tax compliance software and proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

descriptionByLocale.it:
We are seeking a highly skilled U.S. Tax Manager to lead and manage complex tax engagements for our private clients. In this role, you will leverage your comprehensive understanding of U.S. tax regulations and financial planning to provide strategic tax planning and compliance services tailored to high-net-worth individuals. Your ability to interpret complex tax data will be essential in navigating the ever-evolving tax landscape for our clients.

In this role, you will:
Build and maintain strong relationships with private clients, understanding their unique financial needs and delivering tailored tax solutions.
Develop and implement tax planning strategies that align with client objectives and industry standards, maximizing tax efficiencies for high-net-worth individuals.
Oversee the preparation and review of complex tax returns, ensuring compliance with U.S. tax regulations and internal standards.
Provide expert guidance on complex tax issues, leveraging your in-depth knowledge of U.S. tax regulations and their application to private client scenarios.
Interpret and analyze tax data to generate insightful reports and recommendations, assisting clients in making informed financial decisions.

A minimum of 5 years of U.S. taxation experience with an international accounting firm or similar, specializing in U.S. compliance and consulting tax issues for high-net-worth individuals.
A bachelor's degree with a specialization in accounting/taxation or a law degree from an accredited university.
CPA or IRS Enrolled Agent qualification.
Extensive technical skills relevant to private clients, coupled with a successful track record in identifying client needs and pursuing new service opportunities.
Proven leadership experience, including establishing direction, motivating team members, and fostering innovation.
Demonstrated success in generating new business, writing proposals, and collaborating across engagements and business units.
Advanced knowledge of tax compliance software and proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

jobs.title: Career Start in Audit – Insurance – Oktober 2026
jobs.description:
Du bist Teil eines Teams, das nicht nur Zahlen prüft, sondern Zusammenhänge erkennt. Bei PwC im Bereich Audit - Financial Services (Versicherungen) analysierst du Unternehmen: Wie erfolgreich sind sie? Wo liegen Chancen und Risiken? Wie können sie nachhaltiger werden?

Hier arbeitest du mit neugierigen und ambitionierten Menschen, um die Wirtschaft von morgen zu gestalten – digital, nachhaltig und global. Jedes Projekt ist einzigartig und bietet dir die Chance, zu wachsen und einen Unterschied zu machen.

Vielfalt ist unsere Stärke. Deine Perspektive zählt, und wir unterstützen dich, deine Ziele zu erreichen.

Du willst nicht einfach nur einen Job, sondern etwas bewirken? Willkommen bei PwC! Hier kannst du:Unternehmen verstehen und transformieren: Prüfe Konzern- und Jahresabschlüsse und stelle sicher, dass diese den regulatorischen Anforderungen entsprechen - vor Ort, im Office oder flexibel aus dem Homeoffice.
Digital die Zukunft gestalten: Nutze modernste Tools, KI und Datenanalyse, um die Wirtschaftsprüfung auf ein neues Level zu heben. Deine Ideen zählen!
Nachhaltigkeit vorantreiben: Unterstütze bei der Prüfung von ESG-Berichten und begleite Unternehmen auf ihrem Weg zu mehr Umweltbewusstsein und sozialer Verantwortung.
Netzwerke aufbauen: Arbeite mit Führungsteams und CFOs zusammen und knüpfe Kontakte, die deine Karriere prägen werden.
Verantwortung übernehmen: Entwickle deine Leadership-Skills in einem internationalen Team, das dich fördert und inspiriert. Besonders in der intensiven Busy Season von Januar bis Ostern leistest du einen entscheidenden Beitrag zum Erfolg unserer Kunden.

Solltest du dir noch unsicher sein, wie der Alltag in der Wirtschaftsprüfung aussieht, dann probiere unsere Virtual Case Experience aus. In 3-4 Stunden kannst du reale betriebswirtschaftliche Fragestellungen lösen und einen Einblick in die Praxis gewinnen.

Bei uns zählen deine Persönlichkeit, deine Skills und dein Wille, die Welt ein Stück besser zu machen. Was du ausserdem mitbringen solltest:
Einen bevorstehenden oder abgeschlossenen Bachelor- oder Masterabschluss in Wirtschaftswissenschaften, (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, STEM oder einer anderen Studienrichtung.
Begeisterung für Zahlen, digitale Trends und Nachhaltigkeit.
Den Wunsch, in einem internationalen Umfeld zu arbeiten und mit Menschen aus verschiedenen Kulturen zusammenzuarbeiten.
Kommunikationsstark in Deutsch und Englisch.
Teamgeist und die Fähigkeit, Herausforderungen mit frischen Ideen zu lösen.

Deine Perspektive - Mehr als nur ein Job
Wir wissen, dass der Start ins Berufsleben aufregend, aber auch herausfordernd sein kann. Deshalb begleiten wir dich auf deinem Weg - mit einem klaren Plan und starker Unterstützung:Zeitliche und finanzielle Förderung: Wir unterstützen dich umfassend bei deiner Weiterbildung zur/zum dipl. Wirtschaftsprüfer:in. Du erhältst nicht nur finanzielle Unterstützung, sondern auch die Flexibilität, deine Lernzeit optimal zu gestalten.
Flexibilität, die zu dir passt: Unsere Arbeitszeitmodelle geben dir die Freiheit, Beruf, Lernen und Freizeit in Einklang zu bringen.
Mentoring und Entwicklung: Du wirst von erfahrenen Kolleg:innen begleitet, die dich fördern und inspirieren.
Ein Team, das dich stärkt: Bei uns findest du eine Gemeinschaft, die Vielfalt schätzt, Ideen feiert und dich ermutigt, über dich hinauszuwachsen.

Warum PwC? Weil wir wissen, was dir wichtig ist. Sinnvolle Arbeit, die einen Unterschied macht. Ein Team, das dich inspiriert. Und die Möglichkeit, die Zukunft aktiv mitzugestalten.

Bewirb dich jetzt und werde Teil unserer Community of Solvers. Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Zukunft der Wirtschaftsprüfung - digital, nachhaltig und global.

Bei uns zählen deine Persönlichkeit, deine Skills und dein Wille, die Welt ein Stück besser zu machen. Was du ausserdem mitbringen solltest:
Einen bevorstehenden oder abgeschlossenen Bachelor- oder Masterabschluss in Wirtschaftswissenschaften, (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, STEM oder einer anderen Studienrichtung.
Begeisterung für Zahlen, digitale Trends und Nachhaltigkeit.
Den Wunsch, in einem internationalen Umfeld zu arbeiten und mit Menschen aus verschiedenen Kulturen zusammenzuarbeiten.
Kommunikationsstark in Deutsch und Englisch.
Teamgeist und die Fähigkeit, Herausforderungen mit frischen Ideen zu lösen.

Deine Perspektive - Mehr als nur ein Job
Wir wissen, dass der Start ins Berufsleben aufregend, aber auch herausfordernd sein kann. Deshalb begleiten wir dich auf deinem Weg - mit einem klaren Plan und starker Unterstützung:Zeitliche und finanzielle Förderung: Wir unterstützen dich umfassend bei deiner Weiterbildung zur/zum dipl. Wirtschaftsprüfer:in. Du erhältst nicht nur finanzielle Unterstützung, sondern auch die Flexibilität, deine Lernzeit optimal zu gestalten.
Flexibilität, die zu dir passt: Unsere Arbeitszeitmodelle geben dir die Freiheit, Beruf, Lernen und Freizeit in Einklang zu bringen.
Mentoring und Entwicklung: Du wirst von erfahrenen Kolleg:innen begleitet, die dich fördern und inspirieren.
Ein Team, das dich stärkt: Bei uns findest du eine Gemeinschaft, die Vielfalt schätzt, Ideen feiert und dich ermutigt, über dich hinauszuwachsen.

Warum PwC? Weil wir wissen, was dir wichtig ist. Sinnvolle Arbeit, die einen Unterschied macht. Ein Team, das dich inspiriert. Und die Möglichkeit, die Zukunft aktiv mitzugestalten.

Bewirb dich jetzt und werde Teil unserer Community of Solvers. Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Zukunft der Wirtschaftsprüfung - digital, nachhaltig und global.

jobs.title: Director - Cloud, Data & AI (CD&AI) - Generative AI
jobs.description:
You manage engagements with clients and PwC teams, serving as a key leader within our organization.
Act as a trusted advisor to clients, helping them navigate the complexities of AI adoption
Lead workshops and discussions with clients or internal stakeholders to identify AI use cases, understand business challenges, and align AI tailored solutions.
Facilitate your clients' advancement in digitization by gathering their requirements and proposing state-of-the-art solutions.
Anticipate and address potential risks associated with AI implementation, such as data privacy, security, and model performance.
Implement and continuously expand your knowledge of best practices in technology, data engineering, analytics, and consulting approach.

A university master's degree or PhD in fields such as artificial intelligence, computer science, physics, mathematics, or any natural sciences or engineering discipline is preferred.
Over 10 years of work experience evidencing experience with artificial intelligence technologies (such as machine learning, deep learning, advanced statistics and modelling) and more recently in Generative AI (including Agentic AI, GenAI-related cloud technologies, ML engineering).
As experienced hire, you have a solid track record in delivering and overachieving on your projects and you can rely on your network within AI space.
Proven experience in AI leadership roles, with a track record of delivering impactful AI solutions. Consulting experienced is considered a plus.
Proven go-to-market experience within the Swiss market is mandatory, with a strong understanding of local business dynamics and needs.
Fluency in English with a willingness to travel within Switzerland and occasionally internationally; German or French language skills are considered a plus.

jobs.title: Senior Manager/Director in SAP Supply Chain Operations Consulting
jobs.description:
Work with our clients on their supply chain operations processes, evaluate the best use of SAP solutions in Fit to Standard workshops and advise them on design and implementation of best-in-class SAP solutions. 

Help and support our clients to integrate solutions based on the latest technology such as IoT or predictive analytics with their S/4HANA solution. 

You Manage sub-teams, project work streams or transformation programs in challenging and fast paced environments. 

Contribute to the development of our world-class SAP consulting team and expand your capabilities through specific training following your personal development plan. 

Be part of a growing, dynamic team blending SAP innovation and industry expertise and thrive in an environment committed to teamwork, trust and mutual support. 

Be coached by inspiring and challenging leaders.

A degree (bachelor / master / diploma) preferred in business, IT, engineering, business information systems or a digital discipline. 

More than 8 years of experience in process consulting and implementation of SAP solutions with relevant experience in SAP supply chain and logistics modules and a profound understanding of the related business processes. 

You are familiar with SAP Activate and agile- as well as classic project management methodologies 

As Senior Manger you have lead smaller teams of min 10 people. 

As Director you lead at least one transformation program. 

Team player and ready to take on responsibility on projects and to foster innovative solutions. 

Relevant leadership experience which includes coaching, managing engagement teams and client relation management. 

You are able to drive RFPs to generate new business 

Innovative mindset and previous exposure to GenAI and Cloud is advantageous. 

Strong analytical skills with a “can do" attitude, fluent in English and German. 

Flexible to work on engagements in other parts of Switzerland and/or abroad and a valid work permit.

Flexible to work on engagements in other parts of Switzerland and/or abroad and a valid work permit.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Work with our clients on their supply chain operations processes, evaluate the best use of SAP solutions in Fit to Standard workshops and advise them on design and implementation of best-in-class SAP solutions. 

Help and support our clients to integrate solutions based on the latest technology such as IoT or predictive analytics with their S/4HANA solution. 

You Manage sub-teams, project work streams or transformation programs in challenging and fast paced environments. 

Contribute to the development of our world-class SAP consulting team and expand your capabilities through specific training following your personal development plan. 

Be part of a growing, dynamic team blending SAP innovation and industry expertise and thrive in an environment committed to teamwork, trust and mutual support. 

Be coached by inspiring and challenging leaders.

A degree (bachelor / master / diploma) preferred in business, IT, engineering, business information systems or a digital discipline. 

More than 8 years of experience in process consulting and implementation of SAP solutions with relevant experience in SAP supply chain and logistics modules and a profound understanding of the related business processes. 

You are familiar with SAP Activate and agile- as well as classic project management methodologies 

As Senior Manger you have lead smaller teams of min 10 people. 

As Director you lead at least one transformation program. 

Team player and ready to take on responsibility on projects and to foster innovative solutions. 

Relevant leadership experience which includes coaching, managing engagement teams and client relation management. 

You are able to drive RFPs to generate new business 

Innovative mindset and previous exposure to GenAI and Cloud is advantageous. 

Strong analytical skills with a “can do" attitude, fluent in English and German. 

Flexible to work on engagements in other parts of Switzerland and/or abroad and a valid work permit.

jobs.title: Data Security (Senior) Associate
jobs.description:
Are you ready to contribute to the successful delivery of Cyber Technology & Transformation engagements?
This is an exciting opportunity to join our fast growing Cyber Technology and Transformation team. We support our clients along the whole cyber security journey, ranging from technology assessments, identification and implementation of cyber solutions, defining cyber strategy and many more. You benefit from a great team to work with and from our broad engagements enabling you to growth personally and professionally.

As a member of our Cybersecurity consulting team, specializing in Data Protection you will be part of our team of experts on the topic in helping clients secure and protect their most valuable data.
You will work on projects and help our clients to deploy and optimize their data protection strategy.
You will help clients who have adopted new data protection technologies, such as Microsoft Purview or Broadcom, to get the best value from the solution with your hands on expertise in configuring the tool.

3+ years of experience working directly with Data Protection and DLP solutions. Having worked in consulting is considered advantageous.
You have a technical understanding and hands on experience in DLP technologies such as Microsoft, Broadcomm etc
You have experience with Data Protection implementation projects and operational experience running data protection programs.
Strong verbal, written and interpersonal skills. Fluent in English; German and/or French are a plus
A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business Information Systems, Information Technology, Cyber Security, or another equivalent education; professional certifications (CISSP, CISM, CRISC) are a plus.

jobs.title: Data Security Capability Leader
jobs.description:
Are you ready to contribute to the successful delivery of Cyber Technology & Transformation engagements?
This is an exciting opportunity to join our fast growing Cyber Technology and Transformation team. We support our clients along the whole cyber security journey, ranging from technology assessments, identification and implementation of cyber solutions, defining cyber strategy and many more. You benefit from a great team to work with and from our broad engagements enabling you to growth personally and professionally.

As a senior member of our Cybersecurity consulting team, specializing in Data Protection you will lead our team of experts on the topic of Data Protection in helping clients secure and protect their most valuable data.
You will be responsible for driving strategic efforts to expand our client base, fostering partnerships, and contributing to our team's overall growth trajectory.
You will help our clients to implement end to end data protection solutions, identifying gaps and recommending improvements that secure their data and satisfy Swiss and global regulatory requirements.
You will join our extended leadership team in Cyber Security, alongside leading experts in Identity and Access Management, Third Party Risk, Cyber Threat intelligence and Cloud Security.
You will cultivate a collaborative environment within a team of security experts and a global network, focusing on building strong internal and external relationships. Foster a supportive, friendly, and flexible work environment to ensure the success and well-being of our team.
Oversee Data Protection programs, establishing yourself as a trusted advisor to our clients and being accountable for project delivery. Additionally, you will manage our partnerships with Data Protection vendors.
Coach and develop team members on their career path, while contributing to our team growth strategy.

10+ years of experience in Information Security, GRC or related domains. Having worked in consulting is considered advantageous.
You have a technical understanding and hands on experience in various Data Protection technologies such as Microsoft, Broadcomm, and familiarity with technologies such as data masking, encryption, cloud based security, and tokenisation
You have experience with Data Protection feasibility assessments, solution architectures, implementations and operational experience running data protection programs.
You are familiar with the principles of data protection and information lifecycles, and Swiss and key global (EU, UK, US, APAC) data protection regulatory requirements and could perform compliance compliance assessments on topics such as FADP, GDPR, CPRA, PCI, ISO 27001.
Strong verbal, written and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact with all levels of management and staff. Fluent in English; German and/or French are a plus
A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business Information Systems, Information Technology, Cyber Security, or another equivalent education; professional certifications (CISSP, CISM, CRISC) are a plus.
